panels.description: Displays the total count of namespaces that are currently protected by Stork across the selected cluster(s). This metric aggregates all namespaces that are being migrated through **active** migration schedules.
panels.title: Protected Namespaces

panels.description: Displays the distribution of active migrations across different status states in the selected cluster, namespace(s), and migration schedule(s). This panel shows how many migrations are currently in each status category, providing a quick overview of migration health and progress.
panels.title: Current Migrations Status Distribution

panels.description: Displays the total count of successfully completed migrations (including both fully successful and partially successful migrations) within the selected time range.
panels.title: Total successful migrations

panels.description: Displays the total count of failed migrations grouped by namespace within the selected time range.
panels.title: Total failed migrations

panels.description: Displays the percentage of migrations that completed successfully or with partial success status over the last 7 days.
panels.title: Migrations Success% [7d]

panels.description: Visual timeline showing scheduler and storage status changes for all ClusterPairs. Green (Ready) is healthy, Red (Error) requires immediate attention, Orange (Degraded) needs investigation.
panels.title: ClusterPair Status Timeline

panels.description: Displays the distribution of migration schedules grouped by their operational status (active or suspended) across the selected cluster(s) and namespace(s).
panels.title: Migration Schedules

panels.description: Displays the average total migration duration (in seconds) for migrations completed over the last 7 days.
panels.title: Average Migration Duration [ 7d ]

panels.description: Total number of ClusterPairs in the selected namespace(s)
panels.title: Total ClusterPairs

panels.description: Displays the status of the most recent migration run for each migration schedule present in the cluster.
panels.title: Latest Migration Status for Schedule

panels.description: Displays the time elapsed (in minutes) since the last successful migration for each migration schedule and namespace combination. Marks the value in RED if the time exceeds 15mins.

matcher.options: Time Elapsed Since Last Successful Migration

panels.title: Time since last successful migration

renameByName.Value #A: Time Elapsed Since Last Successful Migration

panels.description: This panel shows how many migrations are currently in each status category (Successful, Failed, InProgress, etc.), providing a real-time overview of migration health and activity.
panels.title: Migrations Status

panels.description: Displays the total migration duration (in seconds) for each migration, broken down by migration name and namespace.
panels.title: Migrations Duration

panels.description: Displays a side-by-side comparison of migration durations broken down by stage (volume and resource) for each migration. This bar chart visualization shows how much time each migration spent in the volume migration stage versus the resource migration stage, making it easy to identify which phase takes longer and spot performance bottlenecks.
panels.title: Migrations Duration By Stage

panels.description: Displays the 90th percentile of migration duration (in seconds) for each namespace and stage combination. This metric shows the duration threshold below which 90% of migrations complete, helping you understand typical migration performance.
panels.title: Cumulative Migration Duration by Stage - 90th Percentile

panels.description: Displays the data transfer rate (bytes per second) for each individual migration. This metric shows the throughput achieved during the volume migration stage, calculated by dividing the total bytes transferred by the volume migration duration.
panels.title: Per Migration Data Transfer Rate

panels.description: Displays the average data transfer rate (bytes per second) across all migrations within the selected time range. This metric calculates the overall throughput efficiency by dividing the total bytes transferred by the total time spent in the volume migration stage across all completed migrations.
panels.title: Cumulative Data Transfer Rate

panels.description: Displays the number of volumes in different migration states for each migration. This panel uses two queries to show a comparison between planned volumes (total volumes to be migrated) and successfully migrated volumes, helping you track volume migration progress.
panels.title: Volumes Migration Count

panels.description: Displays the number of resources in different migration states for each migration. This panel uses two queries to show a comparison between planned resources (total resources to be migrated) and successfully migrated resources, helping you track resources migration progress.
panels.title: Resources Migration Count

panels.description: Comprehensive migration overview table showing migration name, namespace, schedule, status, duration, resource/volume counts, and completion timestamp
panels.title: Migration Overview Table

panels.description: Time Since Last Successful Failover Operation
panels.title: Last Failover

panels.description: Time Since Last Successful Failback Operation
panels.title: Last Failback

panels.description: Shows the distribution of actions across different completion states: Successful, Failed, RollbackSuccessful, RollbackFailed, and In Progress.
panels.title: Failover Actions Status Distribution

panels.description: Shows the distribution of Failback actions across different completion states: Successful, Failed, RollbackSuccessful, RollbackFailed, and In Progress.
panels.title: Failback Actions Status Distribution

panels.description: Count of total Failover operations created in the selected time range
panels.title: Total Failover Count

panels.description: Count of total Failback operations created in the selected time range
panels.title: Total Failback Count

panels.description: Status of Failback CRs present in the cluster
panels.title: Failover Status

panels.description: Status of Failback CRs present in the cluster
panels.title: Failback Status

panels.description: Table showing information about Failover/Failback action CRs present in the cluster
panels.title: Failover/Failback Details

panels.description: Count of total number of pods rescheduled by Stork Health Monitor when Portworx goes down on any node.
panels.title: Pods Rescheduled Total

panels.description: Count of pods hyper-converged by stork scheduler i.e scheduled on node where replicas for all pod volumes exists.
panels.title: Hyperconverged Pods

panels.description: Count of pods which are placed on driver node but not on node where pod volume replicas exists.
panels.title: Non-Hyperconverged Pods

panels.description: Displays the current heap memory allocated and in use by each Stork pod (in MiB). This metric shows the real-time memory consumption of allocated heap objects, including both reachable objects and unreachable objects that the garbage collector has not yet freed.
panels.title: Memory Usage per Stork Pod (MB)

panels.description: Displays the CPU usage (in cores) for each Stork pod over a time window. Values represent the number of CPU cores consumed (e.g., 0.5 = 50% of one core, 2.0 = two full cores).
panels.title: CPU Usage per Pod

panels.description: Displays the rate of heap memory allocation growth per pod over a 10-minute window. Consistently positive values indicate potential memory leaks that could lead to OOM kills, while oscillating values around zero indicate normal GC behavior.
panels.title: Stork Memory Heap Growth Rate [10m]

panels.description: Displays the uptime (in hours) for each Stork pod since its last start. Low uptime values (<1 hour) or frequent resets indicate pod restarts due to crashes, OOM kills, or liveness probe failures. Consistent uptime across all pods indicates stable operation, while divergent values suggest individual pod issues requiring investigation.
panels.title: Stork Pod Uptime [Hours]

panels.description: Displays the number of active goroutines in each Stork pod. Goroutine leaks are a common failure mode in Go controllers that spawn goroutines for watching resources, processing migrations, and handling schedules. Unbounded growth (e.g., >1000) or steadily increasing values indicate leaked watchers or stuck operations that will degrade performance and eventually crash pods.
panels.title: Goroutine Count per Pod

panels.description: Displays the 75th percentile garbage collection pause duration (in milliseconds) for each Stork pod. High GC pause times (>100ms sustained) indicate memory pressure that causes request latency spikes. This is critical for Stork because scheduler extender requests have tight timeout requirements - prolonged GC pauses can cause scheduling failures and degraded performance.
panels.title: GC Pause Duration (P75)
